serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of cooked barley in Singapore is approximately 200g, served as a warm grain dish or as part of a barley drink preparation.

nutrient_source.title: Barley — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Barley (cr_id G00011007). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 where not available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Barley, pearled, boiled
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pearl barley (boiled) has a well-established GI of approximately 25 (low GI) per the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per 200g serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving / 100 = 25 × 54.46 / 100 = 13.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 199mg/100g is borderline; beta-glucan fibre at 3.7g/100g supports arterial health
hypertension.guidance: Barley's beta-glucan fibre (3.7g/100g) supports blood pressure management, but sodium at 199mg/100g is borderline high — avoid adding salt and request no added sugar if served as a sweetened drink.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~25) driven by high beta-glucan fibre content; sugar very low at 0.28g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: Barley has a low glycaemic index of approximately 25 due to its beta-glucan fibre content, which slows glucose absorption and improves insulin sensitivity; control portion size as carbohydrate is 27.2g/100g, and avoid sweetened preparations.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Beta-glucan soluble fibre reduces LDL cholesterol; low sugar and moderate fat profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Barley's beta-glucan soluble fibre (3.7g/100g) is clinically recognised for LDL cholesterol reduction; the low sugar (0.28g/100g) and moderate fat (2.74g/100g) profile make it a heart-friendly grain when prepared without added sugar or salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dietary fibre >3g/100g from whole-food source supports gut microbiome; however, no direct omega-3 or strong anti-inflammatory micronutrient present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Barley provides whole-food dietary fibre (3.7g/100g) that supports gut microbiome health and immune modulation, but lacks direct omega-3 fatty acids or potent anti-inflammatory micronutrients; it is not unsuitable but should be combined with anti-inflammatory foods for RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Barley (cooked) is a whole grain cereal commonly consumed in Singapore, typically prepared by boiling pearl barley grains in water, sometimes with pandan leaves, and served warm as a grain dish or as the base for a traditional barley water drink. The cooked grain has a soft, slightly chewy texture with a mild, nutty flavour. Nutritionally, cooked barley provides 139 kcal per 100g, with 2.2g protein, 2.74g fat, and 27.23g carbohydrate. Its most clinically significant feature is its high beta-glucan soluble fibre content (3.7g/100g), which gives it a low glycaemic index of approximately 25, making it beneficial for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes. Beta-glucan fibre also supports LDL cholesterol reduction, making barley relevant for cardiovascular disease prevention. Sodium is 199mg/100g, which is borderline for hypertension management. Barley is a good source of iron (1.28mg/100g) and magnesium (21mg/100g). It is naturally gluten-containing and not suitable for coeliac disease. In Singapore, barley is widely consumed as a cooling drink (barley water) and is considered a traditional remedy for urinary tract health. It is vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly.
common_user_queries: Is barley good for high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00011007, confidence 0.95). GI value sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for pearl barley (boiled). All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and supported.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Barley (G00011007) as of 2026-04-16. Cholesterol at 4.0mg/100g is as reported by HPB; this is unusual for a plant grain and may reflect preparation with animal-derived ingredients in the HPB test sample. All core macros (protein_g 2.2, fat_g 2.74, carbohydrate_g 27.23) are non-zero and sourced from HPB. GI of 25 is based on pearl barley (boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2021). Sodium at 199mg/100g is borderline high and likely reflects preparation method (salted water or seasoning) in the HPB sample. Food group reclassified from 'Mixed / Customisable mixed dishes' to 'Grains and staples / Cooked whole grains' to better reflect the food's nature as a whole grain cereal.
